headline: New Highmark Stadium Buzz Dominates Bills News
writeup: The Buffalo Bills' new Highmark Stadium is generating major headlines, from 'outrageous' ticket prices and tailgating costs drawing fan frustration to exciting reveals of bison statues outside the venue and honors for all-time greats inside. One notable absence is stirring controversy: O.J. Simpson will not be honored at the new stadium, drawing criticism from some media figures like Rob Parker who called the Bills 'cowards,' while Bills fans largely reacted positively to the decision. On the lighter side, the Bills joined the NFL's social media pile-on pitching LeBron James on a football transition following his Lakers exit, generating viral attention. Meanwhile, wide receiver DJ Moore is winning over Bills Mafia both on and off the field, including buying an entire store's stock of shirts to give away to fans. With big offseason moves drawing mixed ESPN grades and the 2026 season fast approaching, expectations in Buffalo are sky-high.
